items.title: Brent Sikkema’s Husband Convicted
items.summary: Also: Columbia University’s MFA show, a new Anni Albers biography, and Karla Knight’s cosmic codes.

items.title: Performance Cuts Through the Noise at the Venice Biennale
items.summary: Florentina Holzinger and Miet Warlop transform the Austrian and Belgian pavilions into immersive spectacles of endurance, ecological dread, and controlled collapse.

items.title: A View From the Easel
items.summary: “I feed pistachios to neighborhood crows and jays.”

items.title: Why I Removed Cesar Chavez From My Show
items.summary: Elizabeth Ferrer writes candidly about making “a decision I could live with,” Roberto Lugo's ode to Puerto Rico, and a new Avedon documentary in Cannes.
